name: Sauteed Soft Shell Crabs with Sugar Snap Peas and Hazelnuts
recipeIngredient: 4 small to medium-size soft shell crabs
salt and freshly ground black pepper
1/4 pound fresh sugar snap peas, blanched,
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan melt 5 tablespoons of the butter over moderately low heat.
2. Remove the foam with a spoon and discard it. Transfer the clear butter to a small bowl, leaving any milky-watery residue in the saucepan. Discard the residue. The clear butter is clarified butter and has a higher smoking point now that the milk solids have been removed.
3. Clean the soft shell crabs by cutting away the eyes and mouth, lifting up the top side flaps to pull out the feathery gills, turning the crab over, folding back the tail flap (apron), pulling it away from the body, and discarding it. Place the crabs in a shallow dish and cover with enough milk. Soak for 10 to 15 minutes.
4. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
5. Season each crab with salt and pepper. Dredge each crab in flour and shake off excess. In a heavy large skillet, heat the clarified butter over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking. Carefully place the crabs shell-side down; they may sputter or pop. Cook until they begin to blister, then flip. Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 2 to 3 minutes.
6. In a separate skillet, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter over moderate heat and cook until just beginning to brown. Add the sherry vinegar and remove from the heat. Add the chopped nuts and blanched peas, tossing to coat and combine. Season to taste with salt and freshly ground pepper.
7. Place a crab on each serving plate and top with brown butter sauce, nuts, and peas.
Yield: 4 servings
